symptom: Outdoor fan is spinning but the compressor is not running. No cold air at the supply vents. Suction line is at ambient temp, not cold. Unit sounds normal otherwise.
diagnosis: Failed compressor start relay (hard-start kit) or failed run capacitor. The fan is on its own circuit through the contactor — it spins independently of the compressor circuit, which is why fan alone running masks a compressor fault.
procedure: Kill power at disconnect. Verify with meter.
Open the control panel. Identify the contactor — large black relay with T1/T2 terminals.
Confirm 24V control signal is present at the contactor coil when the thermostat calls for cool (use a helper or a wireless thermostat).
Check the run capacitor first — it fails more often than the start relay. Discharge, measure capacitance. Spec is on the cap label.
If cap is good: test the start relay continuity. Should read closed between terminals 1 and 2 when energized.
Replace with RCD-5 hard-start kit if the original had one, OR a matched OEM start relay.
Wire per the diagram stuck to the service panel cover. Yellow to start terminal, purple to common.
Restore power, thermostat to cool, observe compressor starts within 2-3 seconds of contactor pulling in.
Verify suction line gets cold within 30 seconds.
safety_notes: Fan-only operation for >15 minutes can freeze the evaporator coil in humid conditions — turn the thermostat off until diagnosis is complete.
Start relay contacts arc under load — inspect for pitting before reusing.
Hard-start kits store charge like capacitors. Discharge before handling.
